The student answered correctly, indicating good mastery of electrolyte disturbances related to potassium and diabetic ketoacidosis.
Update knowledge state after this exercise: <高钾血症, good>, <糖尿病酮症酸中毒, good>

Exercise 1657 involves "静息电位" (resting potential) and "神经肌肉兴奋性" (neuromuscular excitability). The question relates to electrolyte imbalance effects on nerve and muscle function, specifically how low potassium affects resting potential and excitability. The student answered correctly, showing good understanding of the physiological mechanisms linking electrolyte levels to nerve function. This builds on previous electrolyte knowledge but focuses more on cellular electrophysiology.
Update knowledge state: <高钾血症, good>, <糖尿病酮症酸中毒, good>, <静息电位, good>, <神经肌肉兴奋性, good>

Exercise 1659 covers "毛细血管" (capillaries), "毛细血管内压增高" (increased capillary hydrostatic pressure), and "慢性支气管炎" (chronic bronchitis). The question tests understanding of edema formation mechanisms, specifically how increased capillary pressure leads to pitting edema. The student answered correctly, indicating good mastery of fluid dynamics and pathophysiology of edema, which is closely related to water and electrolyte balance.
Update knowledge state: <高钾血症, good>, <糖尿病酮症酸中毒, good>, <静息电位, good>, <神经肌肉兴奋性, good>, <毛细血管内压增高, good>, <慢性支气管炎, good>

Exercise 1658 involves "高容量性低钠血症" (hypervolemic hyponatremia) and "水钠代谢紊乱类型" (types of water and sodium metabolism disorders). The question tests classification of water and sodium disorders based on clinical presentation. The student answered correctly, showing good understanding of volume status and sodium balance disorders, which complements previous knowledge on edema and electrolyte disturbances.
